title: AND12002 Circulation Checks  Lower Extremity
text_en: Please watch this entire video before checking the blood flow in your leg.

title: AND12002 Circulation Checks  Lower Extremity
text_en: This video will teach you how to check the blood flow in your leg.

title: AND12002 Circulation Checks  Lower Extremity
text_en: Good blood flow is important for recovery after the surgical procedure on your leg.

title: AND12002 Circulation Checks  Lower Extremity
text_en: Step 1. Look at your foot and toes on the leg recovering from the surgical procedure.

title: AND12002 Circulation Checks  Lower Extremity
text_en: Compare your feet. Do you see any differences?

title: AND12002 Circulation Checks  Lower Extremity
text_en: Your foot and toes on the surgical side should feel warm, be normal in color, and have little to no swelling.

title: AND12002 Circulation Checks  Lower Extremity
text_en: These signs mean you have good blood flow in your leg.

title: AND12002 Circulation Checks  Lower Extremity
text_en: If your foot looks red or blue, feels cool, or has a lot of swelling, your leg may have poor blood flow.

title: AND12002 Circulation Checks  Lower Extremity
text_en: Step 2. Does your foot feel numb or tingly?

title: AND12002 Circulation Checks  Lower Extremity
text_en: A feeling of numbness and tingling may also be a sign of poor blood flow.

title: AND12002 Circulation Checks  Lower Extremity
text_en: Or, you may feel numbness from a type of anesthesia called a nerve block until it wears off.

title: AND12002 Circulation Checks  Lower Extremity
text_en: If you are not sure if you had a nerve block, ask your surgeon.

title: AND12002 Circulation Checks  Lower Extremity
text_en: Step 3. Wiggle your toes. Wiggling your toes may improve blood flow to your foot.

title: AND12002 Circulation Checks  Lower Extremity
text_en: Repeat steps 1 through 3 every 4 hours, or as directed by your surgeon.

title: AND12002 Circulation Checks  Lower Extremity
text_en: Another way to check for good blood flow is by doing a capillary refill test.

title: AND12002 Circulation Checks  Lower Extremity
text_en: Capillaries are small blood vessels that carry oxygen-rich blood to your toes.

title: AND12002 Circulation Checks  Lower Extremity
text_en: The capillary refill test allows you to see how well blood is flowing through these blood vessels to your toes on the surgical side.

title: AND12002 Circulation Checks  Lower Extremity
text_en: Step 1. Gently pinch the tip of your toe directly over the toenail with your hand.

title: AND12002 Circulation Checks  Lower Extremity
text_en: Step 2. Squeeze until your toenail turns white. Then let go.

title: AND12002 Circulation Checks  Lower Extremity
text_en: Your toenail should turn pink again within 2-3 seconds.

title: AND12002 Circulation Checks  Lower Extremity
text_en: This sign means that you have good blood flow in your leg and foot.

title: AND12002 Circulation Checks  Lower Extremity
text_en: Repeat steps 1 and 2 every 4 hours, or as directed by your surgeon.

title: AND12002 Circulation Checks  Lower Extremity
text_en: Contact your surgeon if you notice. Your foot looks red or blue.

title: AND12002 Circulation Checks  Lower Extremity
text_en: Your foot looks very swollen. Your foot feels cold, numb or tingly, unless you had a nerve block during surgery, making your foot feel numb until it wears off.

title: AND12002 Circulation Checks  Lower Extremity
text_en: Or, your toenail takes more than 2-3 seconds to return to a pink color when doing the capillary refill test.

title: AND12003 Circulation Checks  Upper Extremity
text_en: Please watch this entire video before checking the blood flow in your arm.

title: AND12003 Circulation Checks  Upper Extremity
text_en: This video will teach you how to check the blood flow in your arm.

title: AND12003 Circulation Checks  Upper Extremity
text_en: Good blood flow is important for recovery after the surgical procedure on your arm.

title: AND12003 Circulation Checks  Upper Extremity
text_en: Step 1. Look at your hand and fingers on the arm recovering from the surgical procedure.

title: AND12003 Circulation Checks  Upper Extremity
text_en: Compare your hands. Do you see any differences?

title: AND12003 Circulation Checks  Upper Extremity
text_en: Your hand and fingers on the surgical side should feel warm, be normal in color, and have little to no swelling.

title: AND12003 Circulation Checks  Upper Extremity
text_en: These signs mean you have good blood flow in your arm.

title: AND12003 Circulation Checks  Upper Extremity
text_en: If your hand looks red or blue, feels cool, or has a lot of swelling, your arm may have poor blood flow.

title: AND12003 Circulation Checks  Upper Extremity
text_en: Step 2. Does your hand feel numb and tingly?

title: AND12003 Circulation Checks  Upper Extremity
text_en: A feeling of numbness and tingling can also be a sign of poor blood flow.

title: AND12003 Circulation Checks  Upper Extremity
text_en: Or, you may feel numbness from a type of anesthesia called a nerve block until it wears off.

title: AND12003 Circulation Checks  Upper Extremity
text_en: If you are not sure if you had a nerve block, ask your surgeon.

title: AND12003 Circulation Checks  Upper Extremity
text_en: Step 3. Wiggle your fingers. Wiggling your fingers may improve blood flow to your hand.

title: AND12003 Circulation Checks  Upper Extremity
text_en: Repeat steps one through three every four hours, or as directed by your surgeon.

title: AND12003 Circulation Checks  Upper Extremity
text_en: Another way to check for good blood flow is by doing a capillary refill test.

title: AND12003 Circulation Checks  Upper Extremity
text_en: Capillaries are small blood vessels that carry oxygen-rich blood to your fingers.

title: AND12003 Circulation Checks  Upper Extremity
text_en: The capillary refill test allows you to see how well blood is flowing through these blood vessels to your fingers on the surgical side.

title: AND12003 Circulation Checks  Upper Extremity
text_en: Step 1. Gently pinch the tip of your finger directly over the fingernail with your other hand.

title: AND12003 Circulation Checks  Upper Extremity
text_en: Step 2. Squeeze until your fingernail turns white. Then let go.

title: AND12003 Circulation Checks  Upper Extremity
text_en: Your fingernail should turn pink again within two to three seconds.

title: AND12003 Circulation Checks  Upper Extremity
text_en: This sign means that you have good blood flow in your arm and hand.

title: AND12003 Circulation Checks  Upper Extremity
text_en: Repeat steps one and two every four hours, or as directed by your surgeon.

title: AND12003 Circulation Checks  Upper Extremity
text_en: Contact your surgeon if you notice. Your hand looks red or blue. Your hand looks very swollen.

title: AND12003 Circulation Checks  Upper Extremity
text_en: Your hand feels cold, numb or tingly, unless you had a nerve block during surgery making your arm feel numb until it wears off.

title: AND12003 Circulation Checks  Upper Extremity
text_en: Or your fingernail takes more than two to three seconds to return to a pink color when doing the capillary refill test.
